signedReleases.note: npm package with provenance attestations; binary releases via Microsoft's signing infrastructure.

codeReview.note: Azure team maintains; PRs go through Microsoft internal review.

secretsHandling.note: Service principal credentials passed via env; managed identity is preferred (no secret at rest).

credentialStorage.note: Tokens / secrets live in process env. Managed identity tokens auto-refresh from IMDS.

oauthHandling.note: Azure RBAC + scope is the strongest fine-grained access model in the cloud MCP space — per-resource, per-action, per-condition.

filesystemAccess.note: Server itself doesn't read host filesystem; some tools (blob upload) take a path argument from the agent.

shellAccess.note: No shell exec tools; Run Command on VMs is intentionally not exposed.

networkEgress.note: management.azure.com, login.microsoftonline.com, and per-service endpoints (storage, cosmos, etc.) only.

telemetry.note: Microsoft's standard telemetry — disable via env var.

dataResidency.note: Wherever your Azure resources live. Resource data residency is governed by region selection at provisioning.

defaultPermissions.note: Whatever the auth principal has. With a properly-scoped reader role: small. With Owner: very broad.

destructiveTools.note: Management-plane writes affect billing and configuration. Always gate at the host.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Tool output (resource configs, log content, KV secret values) can carry user-authored content. Lower exposure than browser/filesystem MCPs because most output is structured Azure-resource shape.

supplyChainSurface.note: Microsoft-maintained Azure SDKs as direct deps.

rateLimitRisk.note: Azure APIs throttle per-resource-type. Bulk enumeration on busy subscriptions can hit 429s.

verdictScope: Azure infrastructure operations using a managed identity or service principal scoped narrowly via RBAC.
verdictNote: Use it. Azure RBAC is best-in-class for fine-grained scope — exploit it. Managed identity beats service principal where available. Pair with Conditional Access policies. Host-side approval gates for destructive ops.
